description: Using a callee-saved register without saving/restoring it, silently corrupting the caller's value and crashing far from the cause.
body: Failure mode: an assembly function writes to a callee-saved register (rbx, r12-r15, or x19-x28) without preserving it in the prologue/epilogue, so when it returns the caller's value in that register is gone; the caller then misbehaves or crashes somewhere unrelated. Detection signals: the caller fails after calling the asm function but the asm function itself looks correct; a debugger shows a callee-saved register changed across the call; the bug moves when caller register allocation changes. Do not clobber callee-saved registers unsaved. Safer fallback: save each callee-saved register you use in the prologue and restore it on every return path, verify balanced save/restore in the disassembly, and confirm the caller's registers survive the call.
